sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.49 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: A skew brace is a set$B$equipped with two operations$+$and$$such that$(B,+)$is an additively written (but not necessarily abelian) group,$(B, )$is a multiplicatively written group, and$a (b+c)=ab-a+ac$for any$a,b,c B$. Let$A$be a skew brace with left-orderable multiplicative group. Is the additive group of$A$left-orderable? { V.\,Lebed, L.\,Vendramin} No, not always (T.\,Nasybullov, J.~Algebra, { ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
